name: PayPal MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official PayPal MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with PayPal's payment platform — processing payments, managing orders, handling subscriptions, querying transaction history, managing disputes, and integrating PayPal checkout into agent-driven commerce workflows.
use_cases: Processing payments and creating orders from e-commerce agents
Managing PayPal subscriptions and billing plans from billing agents
Querying transaction history and reconciliation from finance agents
Handling dispute resolution workflows from customer service agents
Creating invoices and managing payouts from accounting agents
Integrating PayPal checkout into conversational commerce agents
not_for: Teams using Stripe, Square, or Adyen as primary payment processor
B2B invoicing at enterprise scale (consider PayPal Commerce Platform directly)
best_when: An agent needs to process payments, manage orders, or handle PayPal-based checkout in consumer or SMB e-commerce workflows.
avoid_when: You're using Stripe or another payment processor — or if advanced fraud detection/enterprise features are needed.

auth.notes: PayPal uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow. Client ID and Secret for server-to-server. Access tokens expire after 8 hours. Sandbox and production environments separate.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox: free for testing; production requires merchant account
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard: 3.49% + $0.49/transaction (US); varies by country and volume
pricing.notes: No monthly fee — transaction-based pricing. Rate negotiation available for high-volume merchants. Sandbox free for development and testing. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Sandbox: 20 req/s; Production: varies by merchant tier

known_agent_gotchas: Sandbox and production use different credentials — never mix them up
Access tokens expire after 8 hours — agents must refresh before expiry
PayPal v1 and v2 APIs coexist — prefer v2 (Orders, Subscriptions, Payouts)
Webhook verification requires signature validation — critical for payment security
Currency handling requires ISO 4217 codes and decimal precision varies by currency
Seller onboarding (for marketplaces) is complex — requires separate OAuth flows
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with scoped tokens. PCI DSS Level 1. Webhook signature verification required. Sandbox/production isolation enforced.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 scoped tokens. PCI DSS Level 1. Webhook signature verification. Sandbox/production isolation.
